title: Artificial intelligence continues driving business deals and debates about its valuation
summary: HP and OpenAI form an alliance, South Korea launches a massive AI investment plan, and the market wonders if the enthusiasm is excessive.

title: Bond markets are energized by big tech issuances and new products
summary: Pimco expands private placements, big tech companies issue bonds in euros, and bank CoCos survive regulatory reform.

title: Middle East tension: US and Iran halt attacks, but Strait of Hormuz remains at risk
summary: Wall Street futures rise after reports of a truce, but naval mines threaten oil traffic for months.

title: Global IPO market gains momentum with tech and energy offerings
summary: Momenta seeks $751 million in Hong Kong, OpenAI delays its IPO, and Spanish firms prepare to raise more than 1.5 billion.

title: Energy sector faces geopolitical incidents and investment opportunities
summary: An Aramco helicopter crashes in Saudi Arabia, INPEX remains on hold due to the Strait of Hormuz, and Magnolia Oil & Gas emerges in shale.

title: Political movements in Argentina, China and peace prospects in Ukraine mark international current events
summary: Argentina appoints a new chief of staff, Chinese propaganda about Japanese militarism spreads, and hopes for an end to the war in Ukraine grow.

title: Institutional and retail investors double down on artificial intelligence
summary: Sovereign wealth funds move toward private markets to invest in AI, while Citi highlights the advantages of open-source models.

summary: UBS raises TSMC's price target, U.S. semiconductor index falls 5%, and expectation grows for SK Hynix's listing in the U.S.

title: Samsung and SK Hynix announce a $1.3 trillion semiconductor investment plan
summary: Shares of both companies fell given the magnitude of the planned spending, as the market digests the news.

title: Tesla deliveries could beat consensus expectations in the second quarter
summary: Sales improve in Europe and China according to Morgan Stanley, and the market awaits the official delivery report.

title: SpaceX executes a strategic shift in its business model, yet the market has not reflected it
summary: Elon Musk's space company would be diversifying its revenue streams while the market has not fully valued it.

title: Tokyo Stock Exchange recovers from initial losses thanks to speculative buying
summary: The Nikkei reduces declines after intervention by short-term investors who bet on futures.

title: Hong Kong Stock Exchange opens higher driven by big tech firms
summary: The market shows a technical bounce, with expectations of a slight improvement for the start of the week.

title: Chinese stock market trades lower due to banking sector and tests 4,000 points
summary: The Shanghai index records slight losses, while investors evaluate technical supports.

title: Macroeconomic agenda focuses attention on Spanish financial markets
summary: Investors closely follow U.S. employment data and European inflation next week.

title: Geopolitics of energy and metals pressures global markets
summary: A Spanish gas hub asks to delay the ban on Russian gas, and Chinese brokers seek to influence the LME to expand their metals business.

summary: Kyocera, Mitsubishi Heavy, Nishimatsuya, Hidey Hidaka and DCMHD record stock gains after announcing solid business figures.

title: Polls show division in Japan over government management and monetary policy
summary: 43% are dissatisfied with the prime minister's explanation about a video, while 58% support the Bank of Japan's rate hike.
